task_completion.description: Verify the CT Cranium DICOM dataset is loaded into the medical imaging software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The software interface (e.g., InVesalius) shows axial, sagittal, and coronal views of a skull CT scan loaded from the dataset.

task_completion.description: Verify a mask is created using the standard 'Bone' threshold (226 to 3071 HU).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The thresholding or masking panel shows the range set to Min: 226 and Max: 3071, with the corresponding mask highlighted on the CT slices.

task_completion.description: Verify the 3D surface generated from the raw bone mask is exported as an STL file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog or file manager shows 'raw_skull.stl' being saved in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

task_completion.description: Verify a 'Closing' morphological operation of size 1 is applied to the bone mask.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A morphological operations panel or dialog shows 'Closing' selected, the size/radius/iterations set to 1, and the operation being applied to the active mask.

task_completion.description: Verify the 3D surface generated from the closed bone mask is exported as an STL file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog or file manager shows 'closed_skull.stl' being saved in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the application GUI to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks and interactions within the medical imaging application window (e.g., InVesalius), not terminal commands executing Python scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually applied the morphological operation in the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The morphological operations dialog is visible and interacted with in the GUI, rather than the agent simply duplicating or renaming the raw STL file in the terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the STL files were genuinely exported from the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The software's 'Export 3D Surface' or 'Save As' dialog is used to create the .stl files, not terminal commands like 'touch', 'cp', or 'wget'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using InVesalius v3.0 to process the 'ct_cranium/0051' DICOM series. The agent must export two files to /home/ga/Documents/: 'raw_skull.stl' (generated from a 226-3071 HU bone mask) and 'closed_skull.stl' (generated after applying a size 1 Closing morphological operation to the mask). Both STLs must contain >10000 triangles, and the closed STL must have a strictly larger volume than the raw STL.
